beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make espresso at home
beats.reasoning: Viktor wakes mid-morning after yesterday's climbing session left him pleasantly exhausted. Low energy (45/100) means a gentler start; espresso ritual (never drip) sets his tone. Yesterday ended at Leschi coords, so he's already home.

beats.activity: Drive to Georgetown clinic via surface streets (SR-520 eastbound closure)
beats.reasoning: SR-520 bridge closed 9am–noon eastbound; Viktor avoids it by leaving before 9am and taking I-5/surface routes. Rainy morning adds 5 min buffer. Leschi to Georgetown is ~4 km, normally ~12 min, padded to 35 min due to weather and road closure prudence.

beats.activity: Morning and early afternoon PT sessions at clinic
beats.reasoning: Steady rain 7am–2pm keeps Viktor indoors. Back-to-back clinic sessions are his typical Tuesday rhythm. The meniscus runner from yesterday's entry likely has a follow-up. Clinic work spans through the rain, ending just as precipitation clears.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby deli or cafe
beats.reasoning: Viktor loved Molly Moon's ice cream yesterday but it's only 58°F and still drizzling at lunchtime. He stays in Georgetown for a quick, warm meal rather than venturing out. Same neighborhood as clinic minimizes travel.

beats.activity: Return to clinic for afternoon sessions; Afternoon PT sessions with clients
beats.reasoning: Rain clears by 2pm; Viktor heads back to clinic for final afternoon appointments. No commute needed—he's already in Georgetown.
Evening commute home after clinic closes. Weather is now clear. Georgetown to Leschi ~4 km, normally ~15 min, padded to 40 min for evening traffic and a deliberate pace (low energy carryover).

beats.activity: Paint in home studio, listen to music
beats.reasoning: Viktor values quiet, creative time as a night owl. After a full day of client focus, painting recharges him. No social plans tonight—yesterday's energy was spent climbing, today calls for introspection.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home
beats.reasoning: Viktor cooks a simple dinner, maintaining his evening routine. Staying home aligns with low energy and his preference for close friends over crowds—tonight is solo recharge time.

beats.activity: Read, sketch, and wind down before bed
beats.reasoning: Night owl Viktor extends his evening with low-key activities. No screen-heavy scrolling, just tactile hobbies (sketching, reading) that let his mind settle. Ends at 23:00 to prepare for sleep.

7-hour sleep span crosses midnight, ending at 6am the next calendar day. Sufficient rest to recover from a steady work day and prepare for tomorrow. Night owl pattern means he sleeps slightly later than average.

diary: Woke up to gray skies and that familiar Seattle drizzle. Made my espresso strong this morning—figured I'd need it with the SR-520 closure messing up traffic. Took surface streets through Georgetown, which actually wasn't too bad. The clinic was steady; a mix of runners nursing shin splints and a couple post-surgery rehab clients. Grabbed a quick lunch at Molly Moon's—their sandwiches hit different on rainy days. Afternoon sessions flew by, then I drove home through the tail end of the rain. The studio called to me, so I spent an hour with acrylics and some ambient music playing. Made a simple dinner, nothing fancy, and settled in with a sketchbook. There's something about rainy Tuesdays that makes you want to move slower, create quieter. Felt good to just be still.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure could've thrown me off, but I leaned into the slower pace instead of fighting it. Taking surface streets meant more time, so I mentally prepared for a full day rather than rushing. Work felt grounded—my clients' recovery journeys kept me present. I skipped any evening plans; the weather and my own energy suggested staying in. Painting and sketching aren't scheduled luxuries for me; they're how I decompress after being hands-on with people all day. The gray Tuesday actually felt like permission to be introspective rather than something to resent. By evening I was genuinely content, not restless.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
